escalatedRisks.risk: Micron 6600 ION 245TB now shipping — QLC nearline era begins 🆕🆕 (SECOND PASS)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — May 2026. World's highest-capacity commercially available SSD. Micron G9 QLC NAND, PCIe5, U.2+E3.L.
escalatedRisks.detail: Micron 6600 ION 245TB officially shipping. 4x capacity jump from D5-P5336 61.44TB. Each unit consumes equivalent NAND to 4x D5-P5336 61.44TB or 2x 122.88TB. Targeting hyperscaler AI data lakes and cloud-scale file/object storage. Confirms QLC demand trajectory is structural. If hyperscaler adoption accelerates, QLC NAND consumption could surpass TLC by 2028-2029. Combined with Samsung 1PB roadmap and 321-layer QLC, nearline all-flash HDD replacement is 2026-2028 trajectory.
categoriesAffected: PCIe5 QLC (new category, not currently tracked)
escalatedRisks.confidence: high (official Micron press release + multi-source confirm)

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung 250TB-1PB nearline SSD roadmap with Scality 🆕 (SECOND PASS)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — 2027 target. Co-development with Scality. New flash type with lower endurance than QLC for read-intensive workloads.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung and Scality co-developing 250TB to 1PB nearline SSDs. New flash type optimized for cost/density over endurance. EDSFF E3.L/E2 form factors. 2027 target release. Combined with Micron 6600 ION 245TB (2026) and 321-layer QLC (2028+), the nearline all-flash roadmap from 2026 through 2030 is now clearly defined. Implication: HDD replacement by QLC/ultra-dense SSDs is accelerating, driving sustained NAND demand growth through at least 2030.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium (Samsung/Scality confirmed, timeline subject to fab readiness)

PCIe5 TLC 7.68TB Samsung $1,800-$4,400 / Kioxia $1,402-$4,348 / Micron $2,250-$5,242 three-tier structure now has dual-source confirmation on the Micron premium tier.

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Q2 +70-75% OVERTAKING DRAM Q2 +58-63% — storage bull center shifting 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW SIGNAL — Day 1. NAND price growth rate now exceeds DRAM for first time in current cycle.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND Q2 contract +70-75% QoQ first time exceeding DRAM Q2 +58-63%. NAND MLC 128Gb .51 (+9.73% MoM) 17th consecutive monthly record. Phison CEO warns shortages could shut down entire consumer electronics companies. Storage bull market center of gravity shifting from DRAM (already at extreme levels, naturally slowing) to NAND (lower supply elasticity, AI training cluster high-capacity SSD demand surging).

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung PM1733 officially discontinued 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — May 2026. Samsung Semiconductor official. PM1733 PCIe4 TLC 7.68TB low-end anchor (,390-,500) supply contracting permanently.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung PM1733 (PCIe Gen4 enterprise TLC SSD series) officially discontinued. This was the 7.68TB tracking low-end anchor. Future supply will increasingly depend on refurb/secondary market. PM1733a (upgraded Gen4) and PM1743 (Gen5) are replacement paths but at higher price points. Implies 7.68TB PCIe4 TLC tracking value will need upward recalibration as low-end supply exits.

escalatedRisks.risk: Phison CEO: NAND shortages could shut down consumer electronics companies 🆕

escalatedRisks.risk: 321-layer QLC NAND H2 2026 shipment — distant supply dawn 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Positive signal but NO 2026 impact. 67% density increase vs current 192-layer.
escalatedRisks.detail: Multiple NAND suppliers plan 321-layer QLC NAND shipments starting H2 2026. Timeline: H2 2026 samples → Q1 2027 pilot → Q2-Q3 2027 mass production → H2 2027 volume available. 2026 procurement decisions should NOT be delayed based on this. Strategy: buy now at current premium, 321-layer QLC benefits materialize 2027-2028.

escalatedRisks.risk: DRAM Q2 contract slowing to +58-63% — high base effect, NOT price peak 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Clarification signal. Deceleration from Q1 +90-95% is normal bull market behavior.
escalatedRisks.detail: DRAM Q2 contract projected +58-63% QoQ vs Q1 +90-95%. Server DRAM (+58-63%) still outpacing PC DDR5 (+43-48%). ABSOLUTE price increase in Q2 may exceed Q1 due to higher base. This is typical Phase 2 bull market — sustained growth at moderated rate, not exhaustion.

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung union vote ratified — strike risk CLOSED 🔴
escalatedRisks.status: CLOSED — May 27, 2026. 73.7% approval, 95.5% turnout. BUT bonus resentment creates new HBM packaging slowdown risk.

escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung's largest union ratified the tentative agreement. 62,616 of 65,593 eligible members voted (95.5%). 46,142 YES votes (73.7%). SGUU (DS-heavy) 80.6%. NSEU (DX-heavy) 21.1%. DS employees get 10.5% operating profit in stock bonuses (~$340K-$540K/person). DX employees get flat 6M won (~$4,000). Strike risk formally eliminated. Samsung warm-down → ramp-up recovery 2-3 weeks (mid-June, possibly delayed by packaging friction). New risk: bonus resentment causing HBM packaging intentional slowdowns.
